Common law and civil law are very different. Civil law is a system of governance in which a county or jurisdiction, usually a municipality or a county, is governed by the laws established by a court of that county. These laws govern and constrain the decisions of the courts to the determination of who or what is an individual. In contrast, common law is the system of jurisprudence in which a case is determined solely on the facts discovered by the parties. The judge will not consider anything or anyone else.
